content.problemStatement:
You are given two integers N,KN, KN,K such that K+1≤N≤2K−1K + 1 \le N \le 2K-1K+1≤N≤2K−1. Your friend is hiding from you a permutation (P1,P2,…,PN)(P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_N)(P1​,P2​,…,PN​) of integers from 111 to NNN, whose elements are written cyclically.

Your friend doesn't tell you the permutation. Instead, he tells you this. For each iii from 111 to NNN he will tell you AiA_iAi​ −-− the number of such integers jjj with 0≤j<K0 \le j < K0≤j<K such that P((i+j) mod N)+1<PiP_{((i+j)\bmod N) + 1} < P_iP((i+j)modN)+1​<Pi​.

Given A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, find any permutation PPP producing them or tell that there is no such permutation.


Input Format
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT −-− the number of test cases. The description of test cases follows.


The first line of each test case contains two integers N,KN, KN,K.


The second line of each test case contains NNN integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.



Output Format
For each test case, if there is no permutation producing array AAA, output −1-1−1. Otherwise, output NNN integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​ (1≤Pi≤N1 \le P_i \le N1≤Pi​≤N, Pi≠PjP_i \neq P_jPi​=Pj​ for i≠ji \neq ji=j) −-− the required permutation. If there are several such permutations, you can output any.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105
**Subtask 1 (50 points):** The sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 200020002000

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case, (3,4,2,5,1)(3, 4, 2, 5, 1)(3,4,2,5,1) produces A=[1,2,1,3,0]A = [1, 2, 1, 3, 0]A=[1,2,1,3,0]. Indeed, there is one element among 4,2,54, 2, 54,2,5 smaller than 333, there are 222 elements among 2,5,12, 5, 12,5,1 smaller than 444, one element among 5,1,35, 1, 35,1,3 smaller than 222, three elements among 1,3,41, 3, 41,3,4 smaller than 555, and no elements among 3,4,23, 4, 23,4,2 smaller than 111.
It can be shown that array AAA from the second test case doesn't correspond to any permutation.
